SchoolProfile.title: SchoolProfile
SchoolProfile.description: Represents the administrative and key descriptive information for the school using the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SchoolProfile entity.

name.description: The official name of the school.

taluka.description: The Taluka (sub-district) where the school is located.

district.description: The District where the school is located.

address.description: The physical address of the school.

contactEmail.description: The primary contact email for the school administration.

contactPhone.description: The primary contact phone number for the school administration.

establishmentDate.description: The date when the school was established.

sportsProgramDescription.description: A description of the school's sports programs and facilities.

healthFacilitiesDescription.description: A description of the school's health-related facilities and services.

Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents a staff member, such as a teacher or coach, associated with the school, involved in sports or health management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

schoolId.description: Reference to the SchoolProfile this teacher belongs to. (Relationship: SchoolProfile 1:N Teacher)

name.description: The full name of the teacher or staff member.

qualification.description: The academic or professional qualification of the teacher (e.g., 'B.Ed', 'M.P.Ed').

role.description: The role of the teacher within the school's sports and health management (e.g., 'Sports Coach', 'Health Coordinator', 'Physical Education Teacher').

email.description: The professional email address of the teacher.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the teacher.

dateOfJoining.description: The date when the teacher joined the school.

Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a student registered as a player in the school's sports and health management system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

serialNumber.description: A sequential serial number for display purposes, not necessarily unique across all records.

name.description: The full name of the player.

standard.description: The academic standard (grade) of the player.

age.description: The calculated age of the player in years.

bmi.description: The calculated Body Mass Index (BMI) of the player.

hasSportHistory.description: Indicates if the player has previous sport history.

sportHistoryDetails.description: Details about the player's past sport history, if any.

medicalConditions.description: Any known medical conditions or emergency information for the player.

AttendanceRecord.title: AttendanceRecord
AttendanceRecord.description: Records the attendance status for a specific player on a given date.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AttendanceRecord entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player whose attendance is being recorded. (Relationship: Player 1:N AttendanceRecord)

date.description: The date of the attendance record.

status.description: The attendance status, e.g., 'P' for Present, 'A' for Absent.

FitnessAssessment.title: FitnessAssessment
FitnessAssessment.description: Stores fitness test results and status for a specific player.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FitnessAssessment entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player for whom this fitness assessment was conducted. (Relationship: Player 1:N FitnessAssessment)

assessmentDate.description: The date when the fitness assessment was conducted.

status.description: The fitness status derived from the score (e.g., 'Fit', 'Need Training').

SportSkillAssessment.title: SportSkillAssessment
SportSkillAssessment.description: Records specific skill assessments and scores for a player in a particular sport.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SportSkillAssessment entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player. (Relationship: Player 1:N SportSkillAssessment)

sportName.description: The name of the sport for which the skill is assessed.

assessmentDate.description: The date when the skill assessment was conducted.

skillOneDescription.description: Description of the first specific skill assessed for the sport (e.g., 'spike' for Volleyball).

skillTwoDescription.description: Description of the second specific skill assessed for the sport.

skillScore.description: The score achieved for the assessed skills in this sport.

HealthIncident.title: HealthIncident
HealthIncident.description: Records details of a health-related incident or medical event for a player.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthIncident entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player involved in the incident. (Relationship: Player 1:N HealthIncident)

playerName.description: The name of the player at the time of the incident (denormalized for quick access).

incidentDate.description: The date when the incident occurred.

description.description: A detailed description of the health incident.

definition.description: Top-level collection for each registered school. The document ID (`{schoolId}`) should match the `id` field within the document. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' (Firebase UID of the creator) for primary authorization.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the school.

definition.description: Subcollection for teacher profiles within a specific school. The document ID (`{teacherUid}`) should be the Firebase UID of the authenticated teacher. Each document includes denormalized 'schoolId' (from parent path) and 'schoolOwnerId' (from the parent SchoolProfile's ownerId field) for authorization independence. This collection serves as both teacher profile storage and a role lookup for DBAC.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent school.

params.name: teacherUid
params.description: The Firebase UID of the teacher, also serving as the document ID for their profile and role.

definition.description: Subcollection for players registered under a specific school. The document ID (`{playerId}`) should match the `id` field within the document. Each document includes denormalized 'schoolId' (from parent path) and 'schoolOwnerId' (from the parent SchoolProfile's ownerId field) for authorization independence.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent school.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the player.

definition.description: Subcollection for attendance records of a specific player within a school. The document ID (`{attendanceRecordId}`) should match the `id` field within the document. Each document includes denormalized 'schoolId' (from grand-parent path), 'schoolOwnerId' (from the SchoolProfile's ownerId field), and 'playerId' (from parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the grand-parent school.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent player.

params.name: attendanceRecordId
params.description: The unique identifier for the attendance record.

definition.description: Subcollection for fitness assessments of a specific player within a school. The document ID (`{fitnessAssessmentId}`) should match the `id` field within the document. Each document includes denormalized 'schoolId' (from grand-parent path), 'schoolOwnerId' (from the SchoolProfile's ownerId field), and 'playerId' (from parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the grand-parent school.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent player.

params.name: fitnessAssessmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the fitness assessment.

definition.description: Subcollection for sport skill assessments of a specific player within a school. The document ID (`{sportSkillAssessmentId}`) should match the `id` field within the document. Each document includes denormalized 'schoolId' (from grand-parent path), 'schoolOwnerId' (from the SchoolProfile's ownerId field), and 'playerId' (from parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the grand-parent school.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent player.

params.name: sportSkillAssessmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sport skill assessment.

definition.description: Subcollection for health incidents of a specific player within a school. The document ID (`{healthIncidentId}`) should match the `id` field within the document. Each document includes denormalized 'schoolId' (from grand-parent path), 'schoolOwnerId' (from the SchoolProfile's ownerId field), and 'playerId' (from parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: schoolId
params.description: The unique identifier for the grand-parent school.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent player.

params.name: healthIncidentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the health incident record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs. The central idea is to structure all school-related data hierarchically under a top-level `/schools/{schoolId}` collection. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
To eliminate hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()` calls in security rules), critical authorization context is denormalized into every relevant document:
1.  **SchoolProfile**: The root document for a school (`/schools/{schoolId}`) will contain an `ownerId` field. This `ownerId` stores the Firebase User ID (UID) of the user who created and primarily administers the school. This field is self-contained within the school's document, making its access control direct.
2.  **All Subcollections (Teacher, Player, AttendanceRecord, FitnessAssessment, SportSkillAssessment, HealthIncident)**: Every document within any subcollection nested under `/schools/{schoolId}` will explicitly include denormalized `schoolId` and `schoolOwnerId` fields. This ensures that any security rule applied to these documents can directly check `request.auth.uid` against `resource.data.schoolOwnerId` or against the `schoolId` for teacher role lookups (e.g., `exists(/schools/$(resource.data.schoolId)/teachers/$(request.auth.uid))`) without needing to perform a `get()` call to a parent `SchoolProfile` or `Player` document.

**Structural Segregation for QAPs:**
All documents within a given collection path share a homogeneous security posture. For example, all `Teacher` documents under `/schools/{schoolId}/teachers/{teacherUid}` have the same access requirements. This segregation simplifies rules.

**Access Modeling & QAPs (Queryable Authorization Patterns):**
*   **School Ownership:** Managed by the `ownerId` field on the `SchoolProfile` document itself, allowing the creator/primary admin direct control.
*   **Teacher Roles:** The `/schools/{schoolId}/teachers/{teacherUid}` collection serves a dual purpose: storing the teacher's profile data AND acting as a DBAC role indicator. The document ID (`{teacherUid}`) will be the Firebase UID of the authenticated teacher. This allows for efficient `exists()` checks in security rules (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/schools/$(schoolId)/teachers/$(request.auth.uid))`) to determine if a requesting user is a teacher within that school, without fetching document content. This approach specifically enables secure and efficient `list` operations (QAPs) for all data relevant to a school's teachers.
*   **Player & Sub-Entities:** Access to player data and their related records (attendance, fitness, skills, health incidents) is granted to the `schoolOwnerId` or to any `teacherUid` (as determined by the `exists()` check mentioned above) within that specific school. The denormalized `schoolId` and `schoolOwnerId` in each document enable these rules to be simple and robust against complex `get()` dependencies.

This structure ensures that operations can be performed atomically (e.g., creating a player without needing to first `get()` the school's owner ID from a parent document) and that security rules remain straightforward and highly performable, facilitating easy debugging and maintenance.
